commands.hypothesis: Tests snipers <= 2 + liq > 100 — clean snipe environment combined with adequate T+30 pool depth. Hypothesis: low sniper count screens out the bot-rush rugs, and the liquidity floor ensures the entry/exit can actually fill.

commands.note: KILLED 2026-05-15 — INVALID at n=120: total_net_sol=-0.324, drop_top3=-3.295 SOL. The apparent +0.16 SOL net (Performance by Strategy panel) was driven by 3 lottery tickets — removing them flips the strategy to -3.3 SOL net. 3rd day on kill list. Lesson: the snipers<=2 + liq>100 combo doesn't actually produce edge after outlier-stripping; the sniper + liquidity signals together must NOT be additive on the win side.

KILLED 2026-05-15 — n=36-51 across recent days, drop_top3=-2.47 to -3.298 SOL, monthly run rate -12.93 to -13.8 SOL/mo. Journal auto_status: DEGRADING/DECAYING. Was in 2026-05-14 kill narrative without action item — escalating today. Hypothesis (snipers<=2 + flow_imb>0.6 would compound clean-snipe + sustained-buy-pressure) is falsified: combining the two signals does NOT add edge over either alone, and the flow_imb>0.6 filter selects a population that systematically loses.

KILLED 2026-05-15 — HIT-KILL: net -2.552 SOL, monthly -40.26 to -70.2 SOL/mo, drop_top3 -3.93. Extreme bleeder, no recovery path. Hypothesis falsified: the sum_abs<60 calmness filter on the bottom-excluded universe selects for the WORST subset, not the best. Calm pre-entry paths on the bottom-excluded population apparently correlate with subsequent dump (boredom signal) rather than steady appreciation. Lesson: sum_abs<60 is NOT universally protective — it depends on the underlying universe. Worked on v17/v18 (calm + dd>-10), failed catastrophically here.

KILLED 2026-05-15 — HIT-KILL: net -1.185 SOL, monthly -18.70 to -25.3 SOL/mo, drop_top3 -2.57. The breakeven stop hypothesis (convert -30% SLs to 0% TOs by moving SL to 0 once HWM hits +20%) FAILED. Two possible mechanisms: (a) trades that touched +20% then pulled back to 0% were going to recover to TP — the breakeven cut them prematurely; (b) the +20% touch is the WHM peak, so by the time we get back to 0% the gap-penalty cost on a stop fill makes the realized exit worse than -30%/total_size_sol. The breakeven mechanic looks elegant but degraded the strategy materially vs the v21-bottom-excluded baseline (which is +4 SOL on similar n).

KILLED 2026-05-15 — HIT-KILL at n=59, drop_top3=-2.802 SOL. Vel<5 + mono>0.66 + dd>-10% triple-stack on path-shape signals did not survive sample expansion. Confirms the v9shadow-vel5-10 and v14-vel5-10-dd-shadow kill pattern: the slow-velocity band (vel<5 or vel 5-10) is structurally negative-EV, no path-shape overlay rescues it. Even with the strongest single-feature signal (mono>0.66) and tail-trim (dd>-10), the underlying band's edge is missing. Don't keep deploying vel<5/5-10 variants — the band itself is the problem.

KILLED 2026-05-15 — n=22-25, drop_top3=-1.710 to -1.75 SOL, monthly -7.99 to -10.4 SOL/mo. The trailing-exit variant (trailingSlActivationPct=20, trailingSlDistancePct=5) consistently underperformed the static-TP version of vel 20-50 + dd>-10% (v20-vel2050-dd-base at +1.12 SOL net). Hypothesis falsified: the static-exit's 15pp gap between raw avg (+20.4%) and opt (+5.36%) is NOT runner-profit left on the table — it's tail-volatility absorbed by the static SL. The trailing exit triggers too eagerly (HWM-5% drop after +20% peak fires often), converting winners into break-even/small-loss exits. Lesson: leaderboard sim raw-vs-opt gap is not always reclaimable by dynamic exits — the static optimum may be globally optimal for that combo.
